metadata.name: SBP Cloud Outsourcing Framework
metadata.description: The State Bank of Pakistan Framework on Outsourcing to Cloud Service Providers sets out minimum requirements for Regulated Entities (REs) to outsource their material and non-material workloads to Cloud Service Providers (CSPs), covering governance, due diligence, oversight, security, data protection, contingency planning, and operational controls.
metadata.classification_reasoning: The SBP Cloud Outsourcing Framework is a mandatory regulatory framework issued by the State Bank of Pakistan for all Regulated Entities (banks, MFBs, DFIs, DBs, EMIs, PSOs, PSPs) seeking to adopt cloud computing services. It requires prior SBP approval for material offshore workloads, submission of CO arrangement details, and periodic regulatory reporting.
metadata.framework_purpose: To set out minimum requirements for Regulated Entities to safely and securely outsource their material and non-material workloads to Cloud Service Providers, while maintaining data sovereignty, operational resilience, regulatory compliance, and protection of customers' data.
metadata.framework_scope: All Regulated Entities (REs) supervised by the State Bank of Pakistan — including Banks, Microfinance Banks (MFBs), Development Finance Institutions (DFIs), Digital Banks (DBs), Electronic Money Institutions (EMIs), Payment System Operators (PSOs), Payment Service Providers (PSPs), and designated PSOs/PSPs — for all cloud service models (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS) and all deployment models (public, private, community, hybrid).
framework_objectives: Define permissible cloud outsourcing arrangements for onshore and offshore CSPs
Establish board-level governance and policy framework for cloud adoption
Ensure comprehensive due diligence of CSPs before engagement
Mandate effective oversight and ongoing monitoring of CSP arrangements
Require contingency planning and business continuity for cloud workloads
Ensure right to audit, access, and information for REs and SBP
Define structured exit planning to avoid lock-in and dependency risks
Govern sub-contracting arrangements and supply chain risks
Enforce user access management and multi-factor authentication
Mandate robust change and configuration management controls
Require formal incident management and cybersecurity response
Protect data security, confidentiality, integrity, and residency
Govern cryptographic key management and HSM requirements
Enable tokenization for sensitive data workloads
Mandate secure network architecture and cloud-specific protections
Require periodic security testing including penetration testing
Establish 24x7 security event monitoring integrated with SIEM
Ensure regulatory reporting to SBP for material cloud workloads
metadata.target_audience: Board of Directors, CIOs, CISOs, ITSC, IT Governance Teams, Compliance Officers, Internal Audit, Legal Function, and Procurement Teams of SBP-regulated entities
metadata.regulatory_authority: State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) — Banking Policy & Regulations Department (BPRD) for Banks, MFBs, DBs, DFIs; Payment Systems Policy & Oversight Department (PSP&OD) for designated PSOs/PSPs
metadata.penalty_for_non_compliance: Non-compliance may result in regulatory penalties, restrictions on cloud outsourcing, enforcement actions, and SBP instructions to restrict or shift cloud workloads as per applicable regulations.
metadata.adoption_approach: Risk-based implementation. SBP approval required before outsourcing material workloads to offshore CSPs. REs must submit CO arrangement details to SBP one month before placing material workloads with CSPs.

sections: G. Due Diligence of Cloud Service Provider
J. Right to Audit, Access and Information
M. User Access Management and Authentication

controls.title: Permissible Onshore Cloud Outsourcing
controls.description: All types of workloads (material and non-material) may be outsourced to reputable onshore (domestic) CSPs.
controls.full_text: All types of workloads (i.e. material and non-material) may be outsourced to reputable onshore (i.e. domestic) CSPs. REs shall give preference to onshore CSPs for outsourcing their workloads. REs shall not process and store their data in unfriendly or hostile jurisdictions.
evidence_requirements: Cloud outsourcing arrangement register showing CSP classification (onshore/offshore)
Board-approved policy defining preference for onshore CSPs
Documentation confirming CSPs are not in hostile or unfriendly jurisdictions

controls.title: SBP Approval for Material Offshore Workloads
controls.description: Banks, MFBs, DBs, DFIs, and designated PSOs/PSPs must obtain SBP approval before outsourcing material workloads to offshore CSPs. Non-material workloads may be outsourced to offshore CSPs without SBP approval.
controls.full_text: Banks, MFBs, DBs, DFIs and designated PSOs/PSPs may outsource their non-material workloads to offshore CSPs. However, outsourcing of their material workloads to offshore CSPs shall be subject to SBP approval whereby SBP may grant approval on case to case basis, after considering: systemic implications, institution specific risks, legal and other strategic risks, data processing and storage, availability and quality of services, security and other controls, contingency and exit planning, resilience, sub-contracting, assurance mechanism, and roles and responsibilities. For approval, banks, MFBs, DBs and DFIs shall submit their request to BPRD whereas designated PSOs/PSPs shall submit it to PSP&OD.
evidence_requirements: SBP approval letter for material offshore cloud arrangements
Application submitted to BPRD (for banks/MFBs/DBs/DFIs) or PSP&OD (for designated PSOs/PSPs)
Risk assessment covering all SBP evaluation criteria
Register distinguishing material vs non-material workloads

controls.title: EMI and Non-Designated PSO/PSP Offshore Outsourcing
controls.description: EMIs and non-designated PSOs/PSPs may outsource their material and non-material workloads to offshore CSPs.
controls.full_text: EMIs, non-designated PSOs/PSPs may outsource their material and non-material workloads to offshore (i.e. outside Pakistan) CSPs.
evidence_requirements: Documentation confirming RE's regulatory classification as EMI or non-designated PSO/PSP
Cloud outsourcing arrangement register
Contract with offshore CSP meeting all framework requirements

controls.title: RE Accountability and SBP Oversight Powers
controls.description: Outsourcing to CSPs does not absolve REs from their prime responsibilities. SBP may instruct REs to restrict cloud outsourcing or shift workloads to an SBP-designated onshore community cloud.
controls.full_text: Outsourcing of services to CSPs does not absolve the REs from their prime responsibilities including managing and running the business operations effectively, legal and regulatory compliance, and protection of customers' data. SBP may instruct any RE to restrict outsourcing of their workloads to CSPs due to its systemic impact, unacceptable risks and any other concerns. REs shall submit details of their CO arrangements to SBP, as and when required. SBP may instruct REs to shift their cloud based workloads to SBP designated onshore community cloud as and when the same is available.
evidence_requirements: CO arrangement details submitted to SBP on request
Policy confirming RE accountability for all cloud-hosted information assets
Compliance attestation by internal audit to Board of Directors

controls.title: Board-Approved Cloud Outsourcing Policy
controls.description: REs shall develop a comprehensive cloud outsourcing policy duly approved by their Board of Directors, covering all aspects prescribed in the framework.
controls.full_text: Develop a comprehensive policy for CO duly approved by their BoD. In this regard, the REs can also amend their existing 'Policy for Outsourcing Arrangements' to include/update CO. The policy shall encompass all services, as per requirements provided in section E above that can be outsourced to CSP, and at least include all those aspects that have been prescribed in this framework.
evidence_requirements: Board-approved Cloud Outsourcing Policy document
Board meeting minutes showing policy approval
Policy review records (at least annual)
Policy coverage checklist against all framework sections

controls.title: Due Diligence and Proactive Risk Identification
controls.description: REs shall conduct appropriate due diligence of CSPs and proactively identify any risks from CO arrangements, including sub-contracting risks.
controls.full_text: Conduct appropriate due diligence of CSPs and proactively identify any risks emanating from their CO arrangements including risks associated with sub-contracting by CSPs.
evidence_requirements: Due diligence reports for each CSP
Risk assessment covering sub-contracting risks
Risk register for CO arrangements

controls.title: Enterprise Risk Management Framework Update for Cloud
controls.description: REs shall update their Enterprise Risk Management framework to include cloud-specific risks: sub-contracting, geographic/jurisdictional risks, legal, regulatory, connectivity, political/security situation, and oversight feasibility.
controls.full_text: Update their Enterprise Risk Management framework or other relevant policies for effective oversight and management of risks emanating from the CO arrangement(s). The framework shall include sub-contracting risks, assessment of cloud service location(s) especially if outside Pakistan with specific focus on areas including but not limited to legal aspects; regulatory issues; jurisdictional concerns; availability; security and resilience of information assets and services; connectivity; political and security situation; ease of oversight; plausibility of RE, SBP and external audit staff to travel for onsite assessment/audit or alternate assurance mechanism.
evidence_requirements: Updated Enterprise Risk Management framework document
Cloud-specific risk assessment covering all required areas
Jurisdictional risk assessment for offshore CSP locations
Alternate assurance mechanism documentation where onsite audit is impractical

controls.title: ITSC Cloud Governance Delegation
controls.description: Cloud-specific governance responsibilities including SLA oversight, KPI/KRI review, and incident oversight shall be delegated to the IT Steering Committee (ITSC) with suitably amended Terms of Reference.
controls.full_text: Delegate cloud specific governance responsibilities such as for overseeing adherence to regulatory as well as performance requirements, including cloud service SLAs, reviewing of KPIs and KRIs, incidents (including cybersecurity incidents) and other relevant matters to the ITSC. In this regard, the ToRs of ITSC shall be suitably amended to include these responsibilities.
evidence_requirements: Amended ITSC Terms of Reference (ToR) covering cloud governance responsibilities
ITSC meeting minutes showing cloud SLA, KPI/KRI, and incident reviews
Cloud governance delegation documentation

controls.title: Legally Binding SLAs with CSPs
controls.description: All CO arrangements shall be undertaken through legally binding SLAs covering all areas in Appendix I, vetted by the RE's legal function, and executed by the RE itself (not parent/subsidiary) with governing law preferably of Pakistan.
controls.full_text: Undertake all CO arrangements through legally binding SLAs, which shall at least include the areas prescribed in Appendix – I. These SLAs must be vetted by the legal function of the RE and be executed by the REs (except branches of foreign banks) themselves instead of their parent company or subsidiary, with governing law preferably as law of Pakistan. However, compliance with the laws of Pakistan, along with compliance to any legal obligation as prescribed by the host country of the CSP, shall be mandatory at all times.
evidence_requirements: Signed SLA agreement with CSP covering all Appendix I requirements
Legal function review and sign-off on SLA
SLA executed by RE directly (not parent/subsidiary)
Confirmation of governing law clause (preferably law of Pakistan)

controls.title: Defined Roles and Responsibilities Before Transfer
controls.description: REs shall define and agree upon roles and responsibilities of IT and operational departments before transferring information assets and services to a CSP.
controls.full_text: Define and agree upon the roles and responsibilities of the IT and operational departments (e.g. requirement analysis, performance testing, UAT, responsibility for data validation, etc.), before transferring information assets and services to a CSP.
evidence_requirements: RACI matrix for cloud operations
Documented roles and responsibilities for IT and operational departments
Pre-transfer checklist showing agreed responsibilities

controls.title: Internal Audit Certification of Regulatory Compliance
controls.description: CO arrangements must be compliant with legal and regulatory requirements, and the Internal Audit function shall provide independent certification to the Board of Directors.
controls.full_text: Ensure that the CO arrangements are compliant with the relevant legal and regulatory requirements, and the IA functions of the REs shall provide independent certification to their BoDs in this regard.
evidence_requirements: Internal Audit certification report to the Board of Directors
Internal Audit review scope and methodology for CO compliance
Board acknowledgment of IA certification

controls.title: CSP Feasibility and Financial Due Diligence
controls.description: REs shall evaluate feasibility of CO arrangements (cost, quality, regulatory compliance), assess CSP's ability to meet Pakistan's legal/regulatory requirements, financial strength, and competence/track record.
controls.full_text: REs shall exercise reasonable care before entering into CO arrangements. Due diligence criteria shall include: Evaluation of feasibility of CO arrangements including cost effectiveness, quality of service, and legal/regulatory/compliance risks. Ability of CSP to meet legal and regulatory requirements of Pakistan. Assessment of financial strength and resources. Competence, business structure, experience, track record in delivering such services.
evidence_requirements: CSP due diligence report covering feasibility, cost, and quality assessment
Assessment of CSP's compliance with Pakistan legal and regulatory requirements
CSP financial statements or financial strength analysis
CSP business profile, experience, and track record documentation

controls.title: CSP Security, Governance, and Cybersecurity Assessment
controls.description: REs shall assess CSP's ability to comply with minimum controls (confidentiality, integrity, availability, resilience), corporate governance, data residency control, and cybersecurity/IT capabilities including international standards adherence.
controls.full_text: Assessment of CSPs ability to comply with necessary minimum controls including physical security/internal controls based on the intended workloads, especially with respect to confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience. Assessment of corporate governance and entity level controls. Assessment of CSPs ability to provide REs the control over data residency enabling them to shift over preferred data center instance, depending upon the cloud service model, in order to host these services at such locations/countries/regions considering geopolitical risks. Cybersecurity and IT capabilities including adherence to international standards and best practices.
evidence_requirements: CSP security controls assessment report
Corporate governance review of CSP
Evidence of CSP's data residency controls and ability to shift data center instances
CSP cybersecurity certifications and adherence to international standards (e.g., ISO 27001, SOC 2)

controls.title: CSP Sub-Contracting, Data Security, Audit Rights, and Certifications
controls.description: Due diligence shall cover CSP sub-contracting risk management, data security controls, audit/access/information rights, support services, contingency/exit arrangements, and up-to-date certifications (IT service delivery, BCP/DR, cybersecurity, Tier III data center).
controls.full_text: Due diligence criteria shall include: Sub-contracting risk management. Data security related controls. Access, audit and information rights of REs, SBP and external auditors of the REs. Support services. Contingency, resilience and exit arrangements. Up to date certification and attestation of the CSPs including but not limited to IT service delivery, business continuity & disaster recovery, cyber/information security, and data center Tier III certification.
evidence_requirements: CSP sub-contracting risk assessment
CSP data security controls documentation
Contractual audit rights provisions covering REs, SBP, and external auditors
CSP support services SLA
CSP contingency and exit plan review
CSP certifications: ISO 27001, SOC 2, Tier III data center, BCP/DR certifications

controls.title: CSP Liability for Security and Service Failures
controls.description: Due diligence shall cover CSP liability for unauthorized transactions, service disruptions, security breaches, and enforcement/penal actions against REs caused by CSP faults.
controls.full_text: Liability of claims/penalties on CSPs for: Unauthorized transactions; Service disruptions; Security breaches; Enforcement and penal actions that may be taken by regulatory and legal authorities against the REs for not complying the regulatory and legal requirements, due to faults by CSPs.
evidence_requirements: Contract clauses establishing CSP liability for unauthorized transactions, service disruptions, and security breaches
Indemnification provisions in CSP agreement
Liquidity damage clauses and contractual remedies documentation

controls.title: Material Workload — Threat and Vulnerability Assessment of CSP Data Centers
controls.description: For material workloads, REs shall conduct Threat & Vulnerability Assessment or equivalent independent assessments of CSP data centers covering physical/environmental security, perimeter security, access controls, monitoring, redundancy, natural disasters, and political/economic climate. Independent assessments required; SOC Reports (level 1, 2 & 3) may be considered.
controls.full_text: For material workloads: Threat & Vulnerability Assessment or equivalent independent assessments of data centers to identify the security and operational weaknesses. The scope of such assessments shall include physical and environmental security, perimeter security, access controls, security & emergency procedures, monitoring, redundancy, natural disasters, and the political and economic climate of the country in which the data center resides. The assessment shall cover all data centers where the REs' data/systems will reside. Independent assessments instead of solely relying on attestations by the CSPs, and the results shall be reviewed by REs' IA as well as Information Security (IS) function. However, the REs may consider SOC Reports (level 1, 2 & 3).
evidence_requirements: Threat and Vulnerability Assessment report for CSP data centers (for material workloads)
Assessment scope covering all data centers where RE's data/systems reside
Internal Audit and Information Security function review of assessment results
SOC Reports (level 1, 2 & 3) if relied upon as substitute

controls.title: Effective Oversight Mechanism for CSPs
controls.description: REs shall develop and maintain an effective oversight mechanism covering CSP performance against SLAs, ongoing viability, cybersecurity practices, changes in service location, sub-contracting, change of ownership, control environment, and timely response to emerging risks.
controls.full_text: Develop and maintain an effective oversight mechanism including but not limited to the assessment of performance against desired service levels and ongoing viability of the CSP and its services, cybersecurity practices and controls, changes in service location(s), sub-contracting, change of ownership, control environment; and timely response to emerging risks and issues.
evidence_requirements: Oversight framework or mechanism documentation
CSP performance monitoring reports against SLAs
Records of CSP viability reviews
Tracking of changes in CSP ownership, service location, and sub-contracting

controls.title: Ongoing CSP Compliance Monitoring
controls.description: REs shall on an ongoing basis review and monitor the CSP's compliance with legal, regulatory, and contractual obligations.
controls.full_text: On an ongoing basis, review and monitor the CSP's compliance with legal, regulatory and contractual obligations.
evidence_requirements: CSP compliance monitoring reports
Records of legal and regulatory compliance reviews
Contractual compliance tracking register

controls.title: Cloud Data and Workload Access Monitoring
controls.description: REs shall monitor access to their cloud data and workloads, wherever possible, such as through cloud activity reports.
controls.full_text: Monitor access to their cloud data/workloads, wherever possible such as through cloud activity reports.
evidence_requirements: Cloud activity reports or access monitoring dashboards
Access monitoring policy for cloud workloads

controls.title: Review of CSP Internal Control and Audit Reports
controls.description: REs shall review internal control assessment and audit reports of CSPs to obtain assurance regarding security and resilience.
controls.full_text: Review internal control assessment/audit reports of the CSPs, in order to obtain assurance regarding the security and resilience.
evidence_requirements: CSP internal control assessment reports reviewed by RE
CSP audit reports (e.g., SOC 2 Type II) reviewed and documented
Review findings and management responses

controls.title: Material Workload — Comprehensive CSP Audit
controls.description: For material workloads, REs shall conduct comprehensive audits of CSPs (themselves or through third-party assessors/pooled audits) at least once every two years, covering infrastructure and related software. In valid cases where onsite audit is not possible, internationally recognized third-party certifications may be relied upon.
controls.full_text: For material workload, conduct comprehensive audit of the CSPs, either themselves or through third party assessors/pooled audits, at least once in two years. The scope of the audit shall at least include the infrastructure and related software used to deliver cloud services to the RE. However, in case where audit/onsite assessment cannot be conducted due to a valid reason(s), REs may rely on internationally recognized third party certifications and reports made available by the CSPs, after sufficient understanding and review of their scope, methodology and the ability of the assessors.
evidence_requirements: Comprehensive CSP audit report (at least once every two years for material workloads)
Audit scope covering CSP infrastructure and related software
Third-party assessor engagement letter if pooled/third-party audit used
Third-party certification reliance documentation with scope and methodology review (if onsite not feasible)

controls.title: Contingency Plan for Cloud Workloads — Design and Redundancy
controls.description: REs shall develop contingency plans for CO workloads. They shall prudently select implementation options for resilience, maximize redundancy by design, implement health checks for High Availability, and ensure redundant connectivity through different international internet cables with contractual penalties for disruptions.
controls.full_text: REs shall develop contingency plan for their CO workloads in order to deal with any disruption/degradation of cloud related services. The contingency plan shall take into account all possible scenarios regarding the unavailability of CSP related services. REs shall: Prudently select appropriate implementation option (service and deployment model, availability zones, server/server-less, etc.) along with related communication technologies, to offer better resilience that commensurate with their workload. Maximize the redundancy by design and workload distribution, and implement health and monitoring checks for ensuring HA of their cloud workloads. Ensure redundant and robust connectivity arrangements through different international internet cables and include penalties for disruption and downtimes in their agreements.
evidence_requirements: Cloud Contingency Plan document
Architecture documentation showing availability zones and redundancy design
High Availability monitoring configuration evidence
Connectivity arrangement documentation with multiple international internet cables
SLA clauses showing penalties for connectivity disruptions

controls.title: Contingency Plan — Roles, Review, and Update
controls.description: REs shall define clear roles and responsibilities for the contingency plan and periodically review and update it, taking into account changes in availability zones, business requirements, alternate CSPs, and technology changes.
controls.full_text: Define clear roles and responsibilities including responsibility for signing off, updating and activating the contingency plan. Periodically review and update the contingency plan, taking into account developments which may affect their feasibility. These may include increase in number of availability zones, changes in business requirements, new viable alternate CSPs, technological changes, etc.
evidence_requirements: Contingency plan with defined roles and responsibilities (sign-off, update, activation)
Records of periodic contingency plan reviews and updates
Change log for contingency plan updates

controls.title: Contingency Plan — Annual Testing
controls.description: REs shall periodically (at least once annually) test the contingency plan against various scenarios including internet/communication service disruption and CSP unavailability. Deficiencies identified during testing shall be recorded and corrective actions implemented.
controls.full_text: Periodically (at least once annually) test the contingency plan against various scenarios including disruption of internet/communication services, unavailability of CSP, etc. Where possible, REs may conduct collaborative testing of their contingency plan with their CSPs. Further, the REs shall ensure that the deficiencies identified during testing are recorded and corrective actions are implemented.
evidence_requirements: Annual contingency plan test results
Test scenarios used including internet disruption and CSP unavailability
Deficiency log from testing with corrective actions and closure evidence

controls.title: Audit and Access Rights for Internal/External Auditors and SBP
controls.description: REs shall ensure their internal and external auditors, independent assessors, and SBP have the right to conduct audits and onsite assessments of the CSP and sub-contractors without restriction.
controls.full_text: Ensure that their internal & external auditors/independent assessors and SBP have right to conduct audits and onsite assessments of the CSP and its sub-contractors, if required. Further, there should be no restriction or prohibition on access to REs' cloud related information assets and services for the RE, its auditors, independent assessors or SBP's authorized staff or such visits are otherwise not impractical.
evidence_requirements: Contractual audit rights clause covering SBP, internal and external auditors
Evidence of no access restrictions in CSP agreement
Audit access exercise records

controls.title: Scope of Contractual Audit and Information Rights
controls.description: Access, audit, and information rights in contractual arrangements shall cover premises, data, devices, systems, networks, security testing results, sub-contractor security testing results, company and financial information, and CSP's external auditors and personnel.
controls.full_text: Ensure that access, audit and information rights provided through the contractual arrangement include where relevant: Premises, data, devices, information, systems, and networks used for providing the cloud services or monitoring its performance — including CSP's (and its sub-contractors') policies, processes, and controls; Results of security testing carried out by CSP or on its behalf; Results of security testing carried out by sub-contractors; Company and financial information; CSPs' external auditors, personnel, and premises.
evidence_requirements: SLA clauses covering all required audit and information rights areas
Access to CSP security testing results
Access to sub-contractor security testing results
Access to CSP financial and company information

controls.title: Third-Party Certification Reliance When Onsite Audit Not Feasible
controls.description: Where onsite audit is not feasible for valid reasons, REs may rely on internationally recognized third-party certifications with adequate scope review. REs shall have contractual right to request scope expansion of certifications/assessments.
controls.full_text: In case, where audit/onsite assessment cannot be conducted for a valid reason(s), REs may rely on internationally recognized third party certifications, and reports made available by the CSP. However, reliance on these third party certifications and reports shall be supported by adequate understanding and review of the scope, the methodology applied therein and the ability of the third party and CSP to clarify matters relating to the assessment. Further, the REs shall have contractual right to request for the expansion of the scope of the certifications/assessments/audits to cover relevant controls and systems.
evidence_requirements: Documentation of valid reasons for not conducting onsite audit
Third-party certification review with scope and methodology assessment
Contractual clause for scope expansion of certifications/assessments

controls.title: SBP Information Requests and Audit Finding Remediation
controls.description: REs shall ensure CSPs timely provide any information requested by SBP and follow up to ensure timely remediation of all audit findings.
controls.full_text: Ensure that CSPs timely provide any information requested by SBP, whenever required. Follow up with the CSP to ensure that all appropriate and timely remediation actions are taken to address any audit findings.
evidence_requirements: SBP information request response records
Audit findings tracker with remediation status and closure evidence

controls.title: Exit Plan — Coverage, Trigger Events, and Continuity Measures
controls.description: REs shall develop an exit plan covering stressed and non-stressed scenarios, defined trigger events, alternative solutions, transition plans, roles and responsibilities, and measures to ensure continuity of critical business services during exit.
controls.full_text: REs shall develop an exit plan by considering the materiality and impact of their workloads outsourced to CSPs. The exit plan shall: Cover scenarios for stressed and non-stressed exit circumstances. Have defined trigger events, alternative solutions, transition plans, and roles and responsibilities including responsibility for signing off, updating and activating the plan. Be periodically reviewed and tested. Implement measures including but not limited to contractual or escrow arrangements, to ensure continuity of critical business services in case of exit.
evidence_requirements: Exit Plan document covering stressed and non-stressed scenarios
Defined trigger events for plan activation
Alternative CSP/solution documentation
Transition plan with roles and responsibilities
Contractual or escrow arrangements for business continuity during exit

controls.title: Data Deletion Upon Exit
controls.description: REs shall ensure complete removal of data including logs from all CSP locations upon exit.
controls.full_text: Ensure complete removal of data including logs from all locations of CSP in case of exit.
evidence_requirements: Data deletion confirmation from CSP upon exit
Data deletion clause in CO contract
Evidence of data removal from all CSP storage locations including logs

controls.title: Contractual Termination Rights — Anti-Lock-In
controls.description: CO contracts shall have no lock-in clauses, defined termination rights (ownership change, insolvency, judicial administration, breach, security breach, service deterioration), documented minimum termination periods, and rights to continue arrangement until substitute is arranged.
controls.full_text: To avoid the lock-in and dependency risks, REs shall in their CO arrangement contracts: Avoid inclusion of any lock-in clause or exclusivity arrangements. Ensure right to terminate the CO agreement in at least the following circumstances: Change in ownership of the CSP; Insolvency or liquidation of the CSP; CSP goes into judicial administration; CSP is in breach of applicable laws, regulations or contractual provisions; Significant and material breach of security or confidentiality; Demonstrable deterioration to perform the contracted service. Ensure that the minimum termination period is documented in their CO contracts.
evidence_requirements: CO contract confirming no lock-in or exclusivity clauses
Termination rights clause covering all six required circumstances
Minimum termination period documented in contract
Right to continue arrangement until substitute is available (Appendix I item 30)

controls.title: Sub-Contractor Visibility and Impact Assessment
controls.description: For material sub-contracting, REs shall maintain visibility of the CSP supply chain by ensuring an updated sub-contractor list is provided, and shall assess the potential impact of large/complex sub-contracting on operational resilience.
controls.full_text: The CO arrangements expose REs to various risks relating to sub-contracting. For material sub-contracting, REs shall: Have visibility of the CSP supply chain by ensuring that the CSP maintains and provides an updated list of its sub-contractors. Consider potential impact of large, complex sub-contracting by the CSPs on their operational resilience, and ability to oversee and monitor the emanating risks.
evidence_requirements: Updated sub-contractor list provided by CSP
Sub-contractor impact assessment on operational resilience
Supply chain visibility documentation

controls.title: Sub-Contracting Approval and Compliance Requirements
controls.description: Sub-contracting shall only be permitted if it does not create excessive operational risks and if sub-contractors comply with all legal, regulatory, contractual, audit, and access requirements. REs shall review material sub-contracting agreements and ensure CSPs can oversee sub-contracting on an ongoing basis.
controls.full_text: Only permit sub-contracting by CSPs if such arrangements do not give rise to excessive operational risks, and sub-contractor agrees to comply with all applicable legal, regulatory, contractual, audit and access requirements including granting REs and SBP contractual access, audit and information rights. Review material sub-contracting agreements of the CSP and ascertain that the legal, regulatory, operational, and cybersecurity requirements are complied with, throughout the supply chain. Ensure that the CSPs have the capability and capacity to oversee any material sub-contracting on an ongoing basis.
evidence_requirements: Sub-contractor risk assessment confirming no excessive operational risks
Sub-contractor agreement granting audit and information rights to RE and SBP
Review records of material sub-contracting agreements
Evidence of CSP's capacity to oversee sub-contracting arrangements

controls.title: User Access Management — Four-Eye Principle and Privileged Accounts
controls.description: REs shall implement the four-eye principle for user access administration, periodically review access rights independently, control and monitor service/generic/administrative accounts with MFA and dedicated machines, and create separate administrative accounts with enhanced password controls.
controls.full_text: REs shall implement complete life cycle of user access management for their cloud related workloads: Implement at-least four eye principle for user access administration. Periodically review user access right changes independently. Ensure that the use and access of service, generic and administrative accounts are controlled and monitored. Moreover, the REs shall implement MFA and limit use of these accounts through dedicated machines only. Create separate account of administrative users for routine operations, and implement enhanced password controls (length, complexity, age).
evidence_requirements: Access management policy with four-eye principle requirement
Evidence of periodic independent access rights reviews
MFA implementation evidence for service/generic/administrative accounts
Dedicated machines policy for administrative account usage
Password policy with length, complexity, and age requirements
Separate administrative account configuration evidence

controls.title: User Access Management — MFA, Master Account, Backups, and CSP Access Restriction
controls.description: REs shall implement MFA and IP source restrictions for cloud users, monitor master account usage, implement access controls for data backups and log data, and ensure CSPs do not have access to REs' systems, software, and data.
controls.full_text: Implement MFA and IP source restrictions (wherever possible) for their users accessing cloud environment. Monitor and document the use of master account, and permit its usage only under exceptional circumstances. Implement access controls for data backups including log data, of cloud related workloads. Ensure that CSPs do not have access to REs' systems, software and data.
evidence_requirements: MFA configuration evidence for all cloud users
IP source restriction configuration (where possible)
Master account usage monitoring and documentation records
Access controls for data backups and log data
Contractual prohibition and technical controls preventing CSP access to RE systems/data

controls.title: Change and Configuration Management — Detection and Procedures
controls.description: REs shall implement mechanisms to detect unauthorized changes with automated alerts, document CM procedures mutually agreed with CSPs (including change requests, approval, prioritization, impact assessment, reporting, roles, patching timelines), and ensure CSPs have robust CM controls with advance notification to REs.
controls.full_text: REs shall plan and implement configuration management in conjunction with IT change management. Implement mechanism for detecting unauthorized changes to cloud environment, and configure automated alerts for the changes. Ensure CM procedures for cloud related workloads are documented and mutually agreed with the CSP — including change request and approval procedures, change prioritization and impact assessment, change reporting, roles and responsibilities, timeframe for patching and software releases. Ensure that the CSPs have well-defined and robust CM controls, and notify the REs in advance of the changes.
evidence_requirements: Unauthorized change detection mechanism with automated alerts
Documented CM procedures (change request, approval, prioritization, impact assessment)
Mutually agreed CM procedures with CSP
Evidence of CSP advance notification for changes

controls.title: Change and Configuration Management — Testing, Segregation, and Baselines
controls.description: Changes shall be tested before production implementation. Roles for configuration management shall be defined with segregation of infrastructure, security, and application roles. Baselines shall be created and maintained for cloud-hosted systems with periodic review and remediation of non-compliance.
controls.full_text: Ensure that the changes are tested before their implementation on the production environment. Define roles and responsibilities of REs staff for configuration management of the cloud environment, and at least segregate infrastructure, security and application roles. Create and maintain baselines for cloud hosted systems, and periodically review, monitor, report and remediate non-compliance with the baselines.
evidence_requirements: Change testing records (UAT/pre-production) before production deployment
Role segregation policy for cloud configuration management
Configuration baselines for cloud-hosted systems
Periodic compliance review reports against baselines with remediation records

controls.title: Incident Management — Procedures, SIRT, and CSP Access
controls.description: REs shall define and document escalation, notification, containment, and closure procedures for IT and cyber incidents in consensus with CSPs. A Security Incident Response Team (SIRT) shall be designated with formalized roles. CSPs shall provide access to incident and root cause analysis reports and assist in investigations.
controls.full_text: For incident management, REs shall comply with the following requirements: Define and document criteria, performance requirements and procedures for escalation, notification, containment and closure of incidents (including IT, cyber incidents) in consensus with the CSP(s). Ensure access to incident and root cause analysis reports of the CSPs. Designate a SIRT to provide timely response to IT/cyber incidents. In this regard, roles and responsibilities of CSP and REs' teams shall be formalized. Ensure that CSPs shall provide reasonable access to necessary information to assist in any REs' investigation arising due to an incident in the cloud.
evidence_requirements: Incident response procedures document covering escalation, notification, containment, and closure
Documented SIRT with formalized roles and responsibilities
Access to CSP incident and root cause analysis reports
CSP contractual obligation to assist in investigations

controls.title: Incident Management — Post-Incident Review, ITSC Oversight, and Annual Testing
controls.description: CSPs shall conduct formal post-incident reviews for material cloud incidents. ITSC shall review and discuss cloud-related incidents as part of oversight. The Incident Response Plan shall be reviewed and tested at least once annually with cybersecurity as a key consideration.
controls.full_text: Ensure that the CSPs conduct formal post incident review of the material cloud related incidents and provide their report to the RE. Ensure that ITSC as part of their oversight reviews and discusses cloud related incidents. Periodically review and test Incident Response Plan of cloud related workloads at least once annually, keeping in view cybersecurity as one of key considerations.
evidence_requirements: CSP post-incident review reports for material incidents
ITSC meeting minutes showing cloud incident reviews
Annual Incident Response Plan test results
Cybersecurity considerations documented in IRP testing

controls.title: Data Security — Encryption, Desensitization, Segregation, PII, and Commercial Use
controls.description: REs shall encrypt data at rest and in transit using strong, non-obsolete cryptographic algorithms. Production data shall be desensitized before use in non-production environments. Cloud data shall be clearly identifiable and segregated. Appropriate PII protection measures and Pakistan law compliance are mandatory. CSPs shall not use RE data for commercial purposes.
controls.full_text: Outsourcing of the workloads to the CSPs does not relieve the REs from the responsibility of safeguarding data confidentiality and integrity. REs shall: Encrypt data at rest (including backups) and in transit using strong and non-obsolete cryptographic algorithms. Desensitize the production data before porting or using it on non-production environment(s). Ensure that their data in the cloud environment is clearly identifiable and segregated. Take appropriate measures for the protection of Personally Identifiable Information (PII); and ensure compliance with the requirements of laws of Pakistan at all times. Further, REs shall ensure that CSPs do not disclose their data to any third party including foreign governments/courts/law enforcement agencies without their consent. Ensure that CSP does not use their data for any commercial purposes.
evidence_requirements: Encryption configuration for data at rest and in transit
Data desensitization/masking procedures for non-production environments
Data tagging and segregation evidence in cloud environment
PII protection measures and Privacy Impact Assessment
Contractual prohibition on CSP disclosing data to third parties without RE consent
Contractual prohibition on CSP using RE data for commercial purposes

controls.title: Data Security — Backup, Exfiltration Controls, Data Location, and Post-Exit Deletion
controls.description: REs shall implement backup and restoration testing (at least half-yearly with ransomware protection), implement controls to prevent unauthorized data exfiltration, ensure notification rights for data location changes, and ensure data deletion from all CSP locations upon exit.
controls.full_text: Implement reasonable backup and restoration testing mechanism, depending on the nature of the workloads in compliance with the defined RPOs. Further, the backup mechanism shall have ransomware protection, and the backup restoration testing exercise shall be conducted at least on a half-yearly basis. Classify information assets in terms of their sensitivity, confidentiality & availability, and implement additional controls for high value cloud information assets. Implement controls to prevent unauthorized exfiltration of data from the cloud environment including deployment of content inspection technologies, controls on data downloading and extraction, monitoring unusual data access, etc. Ensure that they are notified about any proposed change in the location of their data, and have contractual right to reject such change, or terminate the CO arrangement on such grounds. Ensure that data is deleted from all storage locations of the CSP following an exit or termination of the CO arrangement, and where applicable, from the systems of any sub-contractor by requesting written confirmation from the CSP.
evidence_requirements: Backup policy with RPO compliance and ransomware protection measures
Half-yearly backup restoration test results
Information asset classification with additional controls for high value assets
Data exfiltration prevention controls (content inspection, download controls, access monitoring)
Contractual right to reject data location changes
Written confirmation of data deletion from CSP (and sub-contractors) upon exit

controls.title: Cryptographic Key Management — Policies, Review, and Ownership
controls.description: REs shall develop policies for cryptographic material lifecycle, ensure expert review of cryptographic algorithms and parameters, document location/ownership/management of encryption keys and HSMs with CSP agreement, and ensure keys are generated solely by REs with sole management authority.
controls.full_text: CSPs use cryptographic controls to secure access and segregate customers' data. REs shall comply with the following: Develop and implement policies and procedures governing the lifecycle of the cryptographic material. Ensure that details of the cryptographic algorithms and other related parameters such as key lengths, renewals etc. are reviewed by a subject matter expert. Ensure that details of the location, ownership and management of the encryption keys and HSM are agreed with the CSP and documented. Ensure that the cryptographic keys are unique and generated only by the REs. Further, REs must have the sole ability to administer/manage these keys and HSM.
evidence_requirements: Cryptographic key management policy covering full lifecycle
Subject matter expert review of cryptographic algorithms and parameters
Documented key and HSM location, ownership, and management agreement with CSP
Evidence that cryptographic keys are generated solely by the RE

controls.title: Cryptographic Key Management — Rotation, Storage Separation, and HSM
controls.description: REs shall periodically change cryptographic keys per international standards, store encryption keys separately from virtual images and information assets, and for material workloads deploy Hardware Security Modules (HSMs) with due controls.
controls.full_text: Periodically change the cryptographic keys in accordance with the international standards and best practices. Ensure that the encryption keys are stored separate from the virtual images and information assets. For material workloads, deploy/implement HSM with due controls.
evidence_requirements: Key rotation schedule and rotation records
Configuration evidence showing keys stored separately from virtual images and assets
HSM deployment and configuration evidence (for material workloads)

controls.title: Tokenization for Sensitive Data
controls.description: Depending on data sensitivity, REs may implement tokenization to minimize data footprint. REs shall assess tokenization solution features and ensure CSPs do not have access or control over the tokenization solution.
controls.full_text: Depending on the sensitivity of data workload, REs may implement tokenization to minimize the data footprint. While implementing tokenization, REs shall assess and evaluate the features and data interactions of the tokenization solution. REs shall also ensure that the CSPs do not have access or control over the tokenization solution.
evidence_requirements: Tokenization solution assessment and evaluation documentation
Technical controls preventing CSP access to or control of tokenization solution
Data sensitivity classification driving tokenization decisions

controls.title: Network Architecture — Perimeter and Cloud-Specific Security Controls
controls.description: REs shall implement perimeter security controls including network IDS/IPS, WAF, and DDoS protection to detect and mitigate security breaches and cloud-specific attacks.
controls.full_text: The network structure and logical layout is of paramount importance in any cloud implementation. REs must implement controls for protection against plausible threats/attacks including cloud specific attacks: Ensure security of their CO arrangements and on premise environments by implementing controls at appropriate locations to detect and mitigate security breaches and ongoing attacks. These controls shall include but not limited to perimeter security, network IDS/IPS, WAF, DDoS protection, etc.
evidence_requirements: Network architecture diagram showing perimeter security controls
IDS/IPS configuration evidence
WAF deployment evidence
DDoS protection configuration evidence

controls.title: Network Architecture — Segmentation and Traffic Routing
controls.description: REs shall implement network segmentation based on workload type and purpose, with a dedicated management network not accessible from operational segments. All internet traffic shall route through a dedicated security segment; other segments shall not have direct internet access.
controls.full_text: Implement network segmentation based on type of workloads (e.g. production, pre-production, quality assurance, development, etc.) and purpose (e.g. end-users, critical servers, other servers, middleware, interface, etc.). In this regard, a dedicated network segment (i.e. management network) not accessible from other operational segments shall be implemented for administration purposes. Further, all internet traffic shall be routed through a dedicated network segment (i.e. security segment) and other network segments shall not have direct internet access.
evidence_requirements: Network segmentation design document
Management network isolation configuration evidence
Security segment routing configuration showing all internet traffic routed through security segment
Network architecture diagram showing workload-based segmentation

controls.title: Network Architecture — VPN/Secure Connectivity and Firewall Reviews
controls.description: REs shall secure cloud-to-on-premise traffic via VPN or direct network connection with stringent access control rules. Firewall rules and access lists shall be regularly reviewed especially after changes.
controls.full_text: Secure traffic between the cloud and on premise environment using a VPN or direct network connection with stringent access control rules configured to ensure routing of traffic from/to dedicated source and destination IPs. Monitor and control access to and security of the cloud environments. In this regard, regularly review the firewall rules and access lists, especially after any changes.
evidence_requirements: VPN or dedicated network connection configuration for cloud-to-on-premise traffic
Access control rules with dedicated source/destination IP restrictions
Firewall rule review records (especially post-changes)

controls.title: Security Testing — Annual Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing
controls.description: REs shall conduct vulnerability assessment, penetration testing, and scenario-based security testing of cloud-hosted systems at least once annually.
controls.full_text: The dynamic and evolving nature of cyber threats requires a high degree of validation and testing of security posture on periodic basis. REs shall: Conduct vulnerability assessment, penetration testing and scenario based security testing of their systems hosted with the CSPs on periodic basis, at least once annually.
evidence_requirements: Annual vulnerability assessment report for cloud-hosted systems
Annual penetration testing report
Scenario-based security testing results

controls.title: Security Testing — CSP Annual Penetration Testing
controls.description: REs shall ensure CSPs conduct annual vulnerability assessments and penetration testing on the infrastructure and applications they manage. REs shall be cognizant of the scope of such assessments.
controls.full_text: Ensure that CSPs conduct vulnerability assessment and penetration testing for the infrastructure and applications managed by them, at least once annually in order to provide security assurance to the REs. Further, the REs shall be fully cognizant of the scope of such assessments while examining the results.
evidence_requirements: CSP annual vulnerability assessment and penetration testing reports
Scope review of CSP security assessments by RE

controls.title: Security Testing — Cloud-Specific Threat Scenarios
controls.description: Security testing shall cover cloud-specific threats including hypervisor jumping, weak APIs, DoS, hyper jacking, wrapping attacks, cloud malware injection, and side channel attacks.
controls.full_text: Ensure that security testing is conducted while taking into consideration various scenarios and threats that are unique to cloud services including but not limited to hypervisor jumping, weak application programming interfaces, DoS hyper jacking, wrapping attacks, cloud malware injection, side channel attacks, etc.
evidence_requirements: Security testing methodology document covering cloud-specific attack scenarios
Penetration testing reports showing cloud-specific threat coverage

controls.title: Security Testing — Vulnerability Tracking and Remediation
controls.description: All vulnerabilities identified for cloud workloads shall be categorized by risk, tracked, and remediated (with post-validation). For CSP-managed infrastructure, alternate assurance mechanisms shall be implemented for timely remediation.
controls.full_text: Ensure that all vulnerabilities identified for their cloud related workloads are categorized in terms of risk, tracked and rectified (including post validated). For the infrastructure and applications managed by the CSPs, REs shall implement alternate mechanism for obtaining assurance that the vulnerabilities are timely rectified.
evidence_requirements: Vulnerability tracking register with risk categorization and remediation status
Post-validation evidence for remediated vulnerabilities
Alternate assurance mechanism for CSP-managed infrastructure vulnerability remediation

controls.title: Material CO — Annual Independent Threat and Vulnerability Assessment of CSP Data Centers
controls.description: For material cloud outsourcing arrangements, REs shall ensure an independent Threat and Vulnerability Assessment of CSP data centers hosting RE data/systems at least once annually.
controls.full_text: In case of material CO, ensure independent Threat & Vulnerability Assessment of CSP's data centers hosting the data/systems of REs, at least once annually.
evidence_requirements: Annual independent Threat and Vulnerability Assessment report for CSP data centers (material CO only)
Scope confirmation covering all data centers where RE data/systems reside

controls.title: Security Event Monitoring — Cloud Native Controls and Automation
controls.description: REs shall leverage cloud-environment controls and tools to enforce consistent security standards and baselines, and implement automated response, remediation, and notification.
controls.full_text: REs shall establish mechanisms for SEM by complying with the following requirements: Wherever possible, leverage the controls/tools available in the cloud environment to enforce consistent security standards and baselines, automated response, remediation and notification.
evidence_requirements: Cloud-native security tool configuration (e.g., AWS GuardDuty, Azure Defender)
Automated response and remediation rules configuration
Security baseline enforcement evidence

controls.title: Security Event Monitoring — SIEM Integration with Cloud-Specific Correlation Rules
controls.description: REs shall integrate CSP-related services with their SIEM solutions for detailed security log analysis. Cloud-specific incident scenarios with correlation rules shall be implemented. AI/ML technologies shall be explored and preferably adopted where available.
controls.full_text: Integrate CSP related services with their SIEM solutions to provide a detailed analysis of the security logs. In this regard, cloud specific incident scenarios with correlation rules shall be implemented. Further, AI and ML driven technologies may be explored and preferably adopted, where available.
evidence_requirements: SIEM integration configuration with CSP log sources
Cloud-specific correlation rules implemented in SIEM
Evidence of AI/ML security analytics tools (if adopted)

controls.title: Security Event Monitoring — 24x7 SOC Monitoring
controls.description: REs shall ensure cloud-related activities are effectively monitored by their Security Operations Center (SOC) on a 24x7 basis.
controls.full_text: Ensure that cloud related activities are effectively monitored by their SOC on 24x7 basis.
evidence_requirements: SOC operational coverage documentation confirming 24x7 monitoring
SOC monitoring scope document including cloud workloads
SOC alert and escalation records for cloud-related events

controls.title: Other Requirements — Capacity Monitoring and Cloud Training
controls.description: REs shall monitor and review cloud workload capacity utilization and provide adequate training on cloud environments to end-users and privileged users.
controls.full_text: REs shall monitor and review capacity utilization of their cloud workloads. REs shall provide adequate training of the cloud environment, to their end-users and privileged users.
evidence_requirements: Cloud capacity utilization monitoring reports
Cloud training records for end-users and privileged users
Training program documentation for cloud environment

controls.title: Security Incident Reporting to SBP
controls.description: All security incidents/breaches shall be reported to SBP per the Enterprise Technology Governance & Risk Management Framework. REs shall investigate root causes, implement corrective actions to prevent recurrence, and fix responsibility.
controls.full_text: All security incidents/breaches shall be reported to SBP in compliance with the requirements specified in the 'Enterprise Technology Governance & Risk Management Framework for Financial Institutions' or as advised by SBP from time to time. Further, REs shall conduct investigations to identify the root cause, take appropriate actions to prevent recurrence of such incidents in future and fix responsibility for such lapse.
evidence_requirements: SBP incident notification records
Root cause analysis reports for security incidents
Corrective action plans and implementation evidence
Responsibility assignment records for security lapses

controls.title: Pre-Placement Notification to SBP for Material Workloads
controls.description: For material workloads, REs shall provide specified information to SBP one month before placing services with CSPs: CSP name and parent company, description of activities and data, commencement/renewal/expiry dates, last contract renewal date, and service and deployment models.
controls.full_text: For material workloads, REs shall provide following information to SBP one month before placing their services with the CSPs: Name of the CSPs, and their parent company (if any); Description of the activities and details of data to be placed with the CSP; Date of commencement/renewal/expiry of services; Last contract renewal date (where applicable); Service and Deployment Models. Banks, MFBs, DBs and DFIs shall provide the required information to BPRD, whereas designated PSOs/PSPs shall provide the same to PSP&OD.
evidence_requirements: Pre-placement SBP notification letter submitted one month before service commencement
Notification containing CSP name and parent company
Notification containing description of activities and data details
Notification containing commencement, renewal, and expiry dates
Notification containing service and deployment model details
Submission confirmation from BPRD (for banks/MFBs/DBs/DFIs) or PSP&OD (for designated PSOs/PSPs)
